# Provider Runtime / Adapter SSOT: Runtime Responsibility

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providerruntimeresponsibilitydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to provider runtime](../provider-runtime.md)

Executable SSOT: [`runtime-responsibility.riido.json`](runtime-responsibility.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `provider-runtime-responsibility-docs`.

This SSOT fixes the C4 Provider Runtime / Adapter responsibility boundary as generated documentation, source anchors, and CI evidence.

The provider runtime owns process/session/raw provider event normalization. It does not own canonical IR persistence, task leasing, client profile presentation, policy interpretation, or Figma UI semantics.

## Executable Assertions

- C4 owns provider process/session/raw event normalization, not canonical IR persistence.
- Agent client and Figma UI concepts are upstream inputs, not runtime adapter responsibilities.
- ProviderCapability and CapabilityFingerprint stay in contracts/C3; C4 consumes them read-only.
- Runtime reuse is pinned by RuntimeID plus CapabilityFingerprint, never by provider name alone.
- Trusted runtime envelopes are provider-specific and require tests before promotion.
